(revenue = $\text{price} \times \text{number sold}$)
questionText: A club plans to sell water bottles. The club members estimate they will sell 96 water bottles when the bottles are priced at \$12 each. subject: math
prompt: A research manager selected 2 random samples of air fryers of a certain type to estimate the average amount of time this type of air fryer takes to preheat to 400 degrees Fahrenheit (°F). The research manager recorded the amount of time, in minutes, each air fryer takes to preheat to 400°F. Based on the first sample, the research manager estimated that this type of air fryer takes an average of 11.8 minutes to preheat to 400°F, with an associated margin of error of 0.8 minute. Based on the second sample, the research manager estimated that this type of air fryer takes an average of 12.1 minutes to preheat to 400°F, with an associated margin of error of 1.9 minutes. Assuming the margins of error were calculated the same way, which of the following best explains why the first sample obtained a smaller margin of error than the second sample?
questionText: A research manager selected 2 random samples of air fryers of a certain type to estimate the average amount of time this type of air fryer takes to preheat to 400 degrees Fahrenheit (°F). The research manager recorded the amount of time, in minutes, each air fryer takes to preheat to 400°F. Based on the first sample, the research manager estimated that this type of air fryer takes an average of 11.8 minutes to preheat to 400°F, with an associated margin of error of 0.8 minute. Based on the second sample, the research manager estimated that this type of air fryer takes an average of 12.1 minutes to preheat to 400°F, with an associated margin of error of 1.9 minutes. Assuming the margins of error were calculated the same way, which of the following best explains why the first sample obtained a smaller margin of error than the second sample?

choices.text: The first sample contained more air fryers than the second sample.
The first sample contained fewer air fryers than the second sample.
The first sample took more time on average to preheat to 400°F than the second sample.
The first sample took less time on average to preheat to 400°F than the second sample.

questionText: The kinetic energy, in joules, of an object with mass 5 kilograms traveling at a speed of $v$ meters per second is given by the function $K$, where $K(v)= \frac{5}{2} v^2$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $K(18)= 810$ in this context?

choices.text: The object traveling at 18 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 810 joules.
The object traveling at 180 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 810 joules.
The object traveling at 810 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 18 joules.
The object traveling at 2,025 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 18 joules.

questionText: A company has a customer rewards program. In March 2020, there were 900 customers enrolled in the rewards program. For the next 18 months after March 2020, the total number of customers enrolled in the rewards program each month was 6% greater than the total number enrolled the previous month. Which equation gives the total number of customers, c, enrolled in the company's rewards program m months after March 2020, where $m \lt 18$?
